title: 3 Opening and Operating a Receiver General Concentrator Holding Account
body_text:
3	Opening and Operating a Receiver General Concentrator Holding Account
3.1	Opening a Receiver General Concentrator Holding Account
Central 1 contacts the Receiver General departmentâs or agencyâs preferred financial institution and provides the required documentation. Your financial institution opens a Receiver General Concentrator holding account to process Receiver General deposits for each federal department and/or agency. The account must be in Canadian funds only. The Receiver General Concentrator holding account functions like a regular chequing or savings account without the agreements and liabilities associated with a standard operating account.
Follow internal procedures for opening accounts. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program, see The Guide (BC), Business Accounts Part, for more information. Your financial institution advises Central 1 when the account is ready to accept deposits and notice to Central 1 to activate the sweep.
3.2	Operating a Receiver General Concentrator Holding Account
Receiver General Concentrator holding accounts are for deposit only. Funds deposited in the account canât be withdrawn for any reason. Only Central 1 is authorized to debit a Receiver General holding account. The account must never be in overdraft.
You canât debit misdirected chargebacks (dishonoured cheques) to the account. See Section 6.3, Managing Chargebacks for Receiver General Concentrator Holding Account, for more information.
You canât charge overdraft charges and/or service against the account. Financial institutions are credited $1.50 per Receiver General deposit at month-end. This credit covers any potential fees that the account may incur.
END
